name: Krummholz Photography
tagline: Wildlife & landscape photography, Colorado high country.
bio: I live in Colorado and spend most of my time above eleven thousand feet, usually before the sun is fully up. This work started as a way to justify the early mornings and has turned into most of what I care about photographing — elk, marmots, pikas, foxes, and the ground they all share.

I don't bait, call, or push anything for a photograph. Almost everything here is the result of getting somewhere early, staying longer than seems reasonable, and letting an animal decide the distance. The landscapes follow the same rule — I go back to the same basins and ridgelines often enough to know what the light is going to do.
I post more regularly, and less carefully, on Instagram.

heroImage.alt: A small herd of elk grazing in an open alpine meadow at sunrise, snow-dusted peaks in the distance, Rocky Mountain National Park.

portrait.alt: Portrait of the photographer on a scree slope above treeline, camera in hand, overcast light.
